items.title: Jamal settles RM66,600 debt to Teresa Kok, avoids auction of belongings
items.summary: This averts the Monday sale of 14 household items seized from his residence. Jamal tendered a banker's cheque amounting to RM66,600 at 5pm yesterday to fully satisfy the outstanding legal costs awarded to Kok, the Seputeh member of parliament. The auction was part of execution proceedings to recover the unpaid sum through the sale of items seized from Jamal's home.

items.title: PRIME MINISTER ARRIVES TO OFFICIATE HAWANA 2026 CELEBRATION Anwar to present HAWANA prizes, special awards to journalists at PICCA Convention Centre
items.summary: Anwar arrived at 2.40 pm and was received by Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah. Also present were Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai, Bernama chief executive officer and HAWANA 2026 Working Committee chairman Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin, Bernama editor-in-chief and deputy chairman of the HAWANA 2026 Working Committee Arul Rajoo Durar Raj, and deputy editor-in-chief (News Service) and HAWANA 2026 project director Mohd Shukri Ishak. The HAWANA 2026 main event was also attended by international media delegates, including those from Indonesia's Antara News Agency, the Vietnam News Agency (VNA), Xinhua News Agency, the Indonesian Journalists Association (PWI), and the Ikatan Setiakawan Wartawan Malaysia-Indonesia (ISWAMI).

items.title: PH under no pressure to name Johor election 'poster boy'
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan will not be pressured into naming a menteri besar candidate just to satisfy its rivals ahead of the 16th Johor election. Amanah Youth executive committee member Nur Hafiz Roslan said the coalition is instead prioritising the selection of a capable leader of high integrity who can unite the state's multiracial population. He was responding to Johor Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who had questioned who PH's menteri besar candidate would be should the coalition form the state government after the upcoming polls.

items.title: Wet and wild afternoon as heavy rain and lightning forecast for most parts of Malaysia
items.summary: Affected areas: Perlis and Penang: Entire states. Perak : Kerian, Larut, Matang, Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim. Selangor : Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat.

items.title: Filipino man arrested with 10 live pangolins, elephant tusk in Sabah raid
items.summary: SWD director Mohd Soffian Abu Bakar said the man was arrested for illegal possession of protected wildlife and wildlife parts. "All the wildlife specimens and parts were seized, and the suspect has been detained to assist investigations under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997. Under the Sabah Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997, offences involving totally protected wildlife carry penalties of up to RM250,000 in fines, five years' jail, or both.

items.title: Muda’s Amira Aisya won’t defend Puteri Wangsa for Johor election, aide named as replacement
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 20 — Muda President Amira Aisya Abd Aziz will not defend her Puteri Wangsa state seat in the Johor election next month, ostensibly to target a parliamentary seat in the next general election. Muda will consequently field Rashifa Aljunied in the constituency. Puteri Wangsa holds deep symbolic value for the party, marking Muda’s first-ever election victory in 2022.

items.title: Winning election remains priority before naming Johor MB, says Loke
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PH's candidate for Johor Menteri Besar, should the coalition win the Johor state election, will be decided later by the coalition's top leadership council. PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke said the coalition's immediate priority is securing victories for its candidates in the election. Also present were Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching and deputy chairman Wong Shu Qi.

items.title: Portugal's unofficial exorcism fever worries Church
items.summary: The scene unfolds not in a church but in the basement of a small shopping centre a short walk from the vast esplanade of Portugal's Fatima shrine, one of the Catholic Church's most visited pilgrimage sites. The gathering was part of a series of monthly so-called "exorcism retreats" held in Fatima that have raised concerns among Catholic authorities, who say they operate outside official Church oversight by self-styled religious figures and risk exploiting vulnerable believers. Marques placed both hands on their foreheads, concentrating silently.

items.title: Toddler injured as wild boar rams into pram in Poland
items.summary: WARSAW: A wild boar collided with a pram in the southern Polish city of Kraków, injuring a 13-month-old girl, German Press Agency (dpa) reported, citing police. The toddler, who had been lying in the pram, had to be taken to hospital with head injuries, the PAP news agency reported on Friday, citing the police. Initial media reports had said an aggressive wild boar had attacked the pram without warning on Thursday on a footpath in the city's Kliny district.

items.title: Heavy rain, thunderstorms expected in three states and Labuan until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit Johor, Sarawak, Sabah and Labuan until 3pm today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), in a statement today, said the severe weather forecast involves Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru. Similar warnings have been issued for Sarikei (Julau), Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Bintulu and Sebauh), Miri, and Limbang (Limbang) in Sarawak.

items.title: Air force invites public to run on Kuantan air base runway tomorrow
items.summary: KUANTAN: Members of the public are invited to experience the rare thrill of running on an active runway at the air force open day celebration here tomorrow (June 21). The air force runway run, held within an operational air base, promises participants an unforgettable experience as they race along a runway usually reserved for aircraft, with air force assets displayed in the background. The runway run is among the highlights of this year's anniversary celebration," he told reporters after opening the air force's two-day 68th anniversary programme at the Kuantan air base.

items.title: Lebanon state media says five dead in fresh Israeli strikes on south
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanese official media said fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday killed five people, despite a new ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hizbollah group announced a day earlier. On Friday, a US official told AFP an immediate truce between Israel and Hizbollah had been brokered by US and Qatari mediators following talks with Israel and Iran. Israel's ambassador to the US said his country would commit to the ceasefire if Hizbollah respected it.

items.title: Woman killed, 1,700 evacuated in beach hotel fire in Dominican Republic
items.summary: SANTO DOMINGO: A woman was killed and nearly 1,700 tourists were evacuated due to a large fire at a hotel in the Dominican beach resort of Bayahibe, local authorities said on Friday. Francesca Valentino, a 46-year-old Italian woman, was killed in the fire at the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel, the DAEH emergency service said in a statement. Those affected included guests, visitors and emergency responders.

items.title: Businesswoman claims lost ownership of condo in alleged gold investment scam
items.summary: She said the problems began after her husband was introduced to an investment scheme in mid-2025 that used property titles as collateral in exchange for investment returns within a specified period. The arrangement allegedly involved several parties, including an investment company and lenders. Ayu said her husband agreed to participate after being assured that the investment was safe and would generate the promised returns.

items.title: Takiyuddin submits notification for Hamzah's reappointment as opposition leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PN chief whip Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan has submitted a notification to the office of the Dewan Rakyat Speaker regarding the reappointment of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader. Opposition member of parliament Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah said the chief whip submitted the notification on June 18, following a meeting with 62 MPs held a day earlier. The 19 Reset MPs include Datuk Seri Ronald Kiandee (Beluran), Zakri Hassan (Kangar), Datuk Dr Ku Abd Rahman Ku Ismail (Kubang Pasu), Roslan Hashim (Kulim Bandar Baharu), Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh), Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz (Tanah Merah), and Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal (Machang).

items.title: Court-ordered auction of Jamal Yunos’s assets postponed after RM66,600 cheque to Teresa Kok
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — The planned court-ordered auction next Monday of 14 seized assets belonging to Umno leader Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos has been postponed, as he has given a RM66,600 cheque to DAP’s Seputeh MP Teresa Kok to clear the legal costs owed to her, Kok’s lawyer said today. Kok’s lawyer Datuk Sankara Nair today confirmed that Jamal had given the banker’s cheque to his client at approximately 5pm yesterday. Sankara said the RM66,600 paid by Jamal is for the legal costs, which includes the RM50,000 awarded cost, interest and allocatur fees.

items.title: US, China, or India? Anwar explains why Malaysia doesn’t have to choose
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, June 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today that Malaysia’s policy of engaging all major powers while maintaining independence and neutrality has increased investor confidence and created new economic opportunities for the country. Speaking at the grand opening of the MKS Inc Super Center Factory, Anwar said that for an emerging economy, the confidence of international investors is essential for long-term economic progress. This strategy of international engagement allows Malaysia to maintain strong ties with global powers despite geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Ops Tiris: Man detained, 1,205 litres of petrol seized in Semporna
items.summary: SEMPORNA, June 20 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 22 Kunak detained an undocumented man and seized about 1,205 litres of petrol during an operation at Kampung Labuan Haji, Pulau Bum Bum, here, Thursday. The suspect and seized items were handed over to the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) Semporna for further action. For information, complaints and inquiries, the public can contact KPDN via WhatsApp (019-8488000), call centre (1-800-886-800), email at e-aduan@kpdn.gov.my or the Ez ADU KPDN application.

items.title: Property worth RM50mil lost in ah long-linked scam, says group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A consumer group claims that more than 100 people have lost property worth over RM50 million in the past five years to a syndicate involving loan sharks, lawyers and civil servants. The Malaysian Muslim Consumers Association said the scheme involved the manipulation of property transactions to transfer ownership of victims' assets. The association's chief Datuk Nadzim Johan said the group had received more than 100 complaints over a modus operandi involving transfers of house ownership titles.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar welcomes US-Iran breakthrough, hopes for lasting peace
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed reports of an initial agreement between the United States and Iran, describing it as positive news for the world. Anwar said any breakthrough that reduces tensions in the Middle East would help safeguard global economic stability and protect ordinary people from the fallout of conflict. Anwar said disruptions in one of the world's most important shipping routes would have far-reaching consequences, including higher costs for businesses and consumers.

items.title: Anwar lauds US-Iran de-escalation, warns geopolitical turmoil hits the poor hardest
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, June 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has hailed the reported de-escalation of tensions between the United States and Iran, describing the breakthrough as a vital win for global energy security and economic stability. Speaking at the grand opening of the MKS Super Center Factory in Bandar Cassia Technology Park today, Anwar expressed relief over the diplomatic progress in the Middle East, a region whose volatility often affects the global economy. The Prime Minister noted that any disruption in the Straits of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil, would have a cascading effect on industries worldwide.

items.title: Bride-to-be among two sisters killed in Temerloh crash [WATCH]
items.summary: TEMERLOH: One of two sisters who were killed in an accident at Km24 of the Temerloh-Jerantut road near Jengka 19 yesterday was due to get married next week. Ahmad Samadi Ibrahim, 55, a relative of the victims' family, said all preparations for the wedding had been completed. In the 7.25pm incident, Dinie Hanis' younger sister, Dinie Nasuha, 19, was also killed.

items.title: Neutrality pays: Anwar links landmark Turkmenistan gas deal to Malaysia’s balanced foreign policy
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, June 20 — Malaysia has secured a foothold in one of the world’s largest gas fields, with Turkmenistan granting Petronas the opportunity to lead development in two major blocks, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today. Anwar said the agreement was struck yesterday during his visit to Turkmenistan, following a series of high-level engagements in Russia. This success, Anwar argued, stems from Malaysia’s strategic neutrality.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Malaysia's neutrality, engagement with all powers boost investor confidence
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Malaysia's policy of engaging all nations while maintaining neutrality and independence is strengthening investor confidence and driving economic growth, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said Malaysia's active engagement with major global powers reflects the country's commitment to a balanced foreign policy that prioritises national interests while remaining open to international cooperation. Anwar said Malaysia's diplomatic approach has translated into tangible economic gains and strengthened international confidence in the country.

items.title: Sabah trade enforcers investigate ‘fish in squid’ claim at KK market
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 20 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) Sabah acted swiftly and proactively following a viral social media post alleging that squid sold at the Central Market here contained fish. KPDN Sabah Director Shahril Nizam Shahidin said an enforcement team was immediately dispatched to the wet market to conduct inspections and obtain an explanation from the trader concerned. He said the trader denied intentionally placing fish inside his squid to increase their weight.

items.title: Why diesel? Anthony Loke defends Shuttle Selatan choice to avoid years of waiting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — The decision to deploy diesel trains for the KTM Shuttle Selatan service was a pragmatic move to ensure commuters have immediate access to transport rather than facing years of waiting, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. With the Gemas-Johor Bahru Electrified Double Tracking project now complete, the Electric Train Service (ETS) has taken over the primary route, leaving the previous diesel fleet available. According to Loke, the Ministry of Transport faced two choices: wait two to three years for 10 new electric multiple unit (EMU) train sets to be manufactured, or deploy existing assets immediately so Johor residents would not be left stranded.

items.title: Horseshoe crabs’ survival triumph: Landings of ancient species in Malaysia increase by 14.44pc this year
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 20 — Malaysia recorded a 14.44 per cent increase in horseshoe crab landings over a three-year period, reflecting the continued resilience of its coastal ecosystems in supporting the survival of this ancient and high-value marine species. The Department of Fisheries Malaysia (DOF) said the total landings of horseshoe crabs increased from 232.80 tonnes in 2023 to 266.42 tonnes in 2025, while their wholesale market value climbed 9.32 per cent to RM2.68 million, compared with RM2.45 million over the same period. “The increase in horseshoe crab landings shows that the country’s coastal ecosystems are still capable of supporting the survival of the species.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Turkmenistan gas deal reflects world's confidence in Malaysia
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Turkmenistan's decision to award Petronas a stake in one of the world's largest gas fields reflects international confidence in Malaysia's stability, unity and economic capabilities, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the landmark agreement underscored the trust foreign countries have in Malaysia's ability to deliver strategic investments through the strength of its institutions, workforce and home-grown corporations. Anwar said Petronas' acquisition of a stake in one of the world's largest gas fields in Turkmenistan was a landmark achievement that reinforced the national oil and gas company's standing among global industry leaders.

items.title: IRB: E-Invoicing drive sees 52,540 taxpayers declare RM4bil in income
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The implementation of e-Invoicing has strengthened tax compliance, with 52,540 taxpayers declaring RM4.07 billion in income, according to the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (IRB). In a statement today, IRB said that since the system was introduced on Aug 1, 2024, more than 230,000 taxpayers have adopted e-Invoicing, resulting in the issuance of 1.505 billion e-Invoices. "Through this approach, 52,540 taxpayers have submitted Income Tax Return Forms for previous years of assessment, declaring total income of RM4.07 billion and tax payable amounting to RM1.009 billion," it said.

items.title: MMEA foils RM2.7mil baby lobster smuggling attempt to Singapore
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said it foiled an attempt to smuggle baby lobsters worth RM2.7 million to Singapore at Tanjung Belungkor jetty early yesterday. Acting Tanjung Sedili Maritime Zone director Commander Mohd Najib Sam said the operation was launched following intelligence from the zone headquarters. He said baby lobsters are a high-value marine resource in strong demand in international markets.

items.title: Disabled couple lose nearly RM50k after fire destroys home
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A disabled couple suffered losses of nearly RM50,000 after a fire destroyed their rented century-old home and source of livelihood in Kampung Hiliran, Chabang Tiga, here two days ago. Yerlianis Nordin, 37, who is physically disabled, said she and her husband, Yusof Ayob, 37, who has dwarfism, were not at home at the time and only learned of the incident after being contacted by villagers. Detailing her losses, Yerlianis said the fire destroyed furniture, electrical appliances, RM1,000 in cash and various important documents.

items.title: Selgate: How Selangor is using private hospitals to cut public healthcare waiting times
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 20 — The Selangor state government continues to expand access to affordable healthcare through its Selgate Healthcare hospital network, in efforts to help the public cope with rising medical costs and insurance premiums. State Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin said Selgate Specialist Hospital Rawang, which was launched last May, is now part of the Hospital Services Outsourcing Programme (HSOP), under which selected patients may be referred to private hospitals for treatment. She said the move would not only provide faster access to treatment for patients, but also ease congestion at public healthcare facilities.

items.title: French curator unearths rare Mozart manuscript
items.summary: PARIS: Musicians this weekend will for the first time publicly interpret music for flute and harp that Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart wrote as a 22-year-old while teaching an aristocratic French student. The 44-page notebook includes a dozen daily exercises the Austrian prodigy gave Marie-Louise-Philippine de Bonnieres de Guines from May to July 1778, as well as seven pieces for flute and harp, he said. Mozart died in 1791 aged 35.

items.title: Senior citizen injured after family member allegedly attacks him with hammer
items.summary: Dungun district police chief, Superintendent Nik Ab Halim Nik Mat, said the incident occurred at 6.55pm, resulting in the 79-year-old victim being rushed to hospital for treatment. "The victim sustained head injuries and was taken to Dungun Hospital for further treatment," he said. "The victim is reported to be in stable condition and remains under treatment at Dungun Hospital," he said.

items.title: LHDN: 52,540 taxpayers declared RM4.07b in income after e-invoicing rollout
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 20 — The implementation of e-Invoicing has strengthened tax compliance, with 52,540 taxpayers coming forward to declare RM4.07 billion in income, according to the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (LHDN). In a statement today, the LHDN said since the system was introduced on Aug 1, 2024, more than 230,000 taxpayers have adopted e-Invoicing, resulting in the issuance of 1.505 billion e-Invoices. “This development reflects taxpayers’ support and readiness to embrace digitalisation in business operations,” it said.

items.title: Anwar arrives in Penang after Turkmenistan visit for Hawana, investment events
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: Immediately after concluding his official visit to Turkmenistan, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Penang today to attend several key events, including the National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 grand finale. Anwar, who is also finance minister, began his one-day working visit to the Pearl of the Orient by officially opening MKS Instruments Malaysia Sdn Bhd at Bandar Cassia Technology Park in Batu Kawan at 11am. He will then proceed to the groundbreaking ceremony for the Setia Fontaines Industrial Park at the Setia Experience Centre in Bandar Setia Fontaines, Kepala Batas, at 1pm.

items.title: RM150 unlimited travel: Rapid KL opens digital monthly pass to all passengers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — Rapid KL has introduced a digital Rapid Monthly Pass, allowing users to purchase and activate unlimited travel directly through the TNG eWallet application. The Rapid Monthly Pass provides unlimited travel for 30 consecutive days across the entire Rapid KL network, covering the LRT, MRT, Monorail, BRT Sunway, Rapid KL buses, and MRT feeder buses. While the My50 Pass is available exclusively to Malaysian citizens, the Rapid Monthly Pass is open to all users, including non-citizens.

items.title: No deaths reported in Bercham storm that ripped up 248 homes, say Ipoh police
items.summary: IPOH, June 20 — A storm accompanied by heavy rain tore that through several residential areas in Bercham yesterday afternoon, damaged an estimated 248 houses across a 1.3 square kilometre stretch, police said. The Ipoh District Police Headquarters said the storm hit areas under the jurisdiction of the Bercham police station at around 3:30 p.m. on June 19. In a statement today, Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said initial monitoring identified five residential areas affected by the storm: Bercham Idaman, Anjung Bercham Utara, Kampung Bercham, Taman Pusat Bercham, and Kampung Tersusun Tasek.

items.title: New cybercrime, anti-racing Bills to join two constitutional amendments when Parliament resumes Monday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — The Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament will begin on Monday, with several key bills set to take centre stage, including the re-tabling of the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2026, which seeks to limit the prime minister’s tenure to 10 years. The sitting, which runs until July 16, is also scheduled to include the re-tabling of the Constitution (Amendment) Bill (No. Attention is also expected to turn to the Cybercrime Bill 2026, as well as the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment) Bill, which is scheduled to be tabled on the first day of the session and debated the following day.

items.title: Wet and windy: Heavy rain alert out Johor, Sarawak and Labuan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has triggered a thunderstorm alert for several regions across Johor, Sarawak, and the Federal Territory of Labuan. According to the alert issued at 10:00 a.m., the affected areas include: Johor : Batu Pahat, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi, and Johor Bahru. WP Labuan : The entire Federal Territory.

items.title: Yadim approaches Sarawak on plans to bring ‘harmonious’ Islamic outreach to state’s diverse society
items.summary: KUCHING, June 20 — Yayasan Dakwah Islamiah Malaysia (Yadim) is seeking to strengthen strategic cooperation with the Sarawak government to further enhance "dakwah" (Islamic outreach) efforts that are prudent, inclusive, and suited to the state’s multicultural society. Hasan said Abang Johari shared his views and advice on adopting a more harmonious approach to "dakwah" that would strengthen inter-racial and inter-religious understanding, particularly within Sarawak’s diverse social landscape. “I would like to thank the Premier for receiving the Yadim delegation.

items.title: Luyang rep: Sabah’s reliance on Danish and Norwegian pork imports not sustainable
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 20 — Sabah should treat pork imports as a temporary measure and urgently strengthen local pig farming to ensure long-term food security and price stability, said Luyang assemblyman Samuel Wong. Wong said a stronger local industry would help stabilise pork prices and support downstream growth in processing, logistics and food manufacturing. “Imports may solve today’s shortage, but only stronger local production can guarantee Sabah’s food security, stable prices and long-term economic opportunities,” he added.

items.title: Doctor, two-year-old daughter among three killed in Pekan crash triggered by puddle
items.summary: Pekan district police chief Supt Mohd Zaidi Mat Zain said two drivers were killed on the spot in the 6 p.m. crash, identified as Dr Mohamad Zekry Zuhairy Mohd Atan, 33, of Segamat Hospital, Johor, and Azaharuddin Mohd Ali, 46, a senior officer with the Inland Revenue Board (LHDN) in Cyberjaya, Selangor. Mohd Zaidi said three family members of Dr Mohamad Zekry Zuhairy, comprising his wife, mother-in-law and father-in-law, were injured in the crash. Mohd Zaidi said all injured victims were receiving treatment at Pekan Hospital.

items.title: Johor Pakatan responds to Onn Hafiz: Focus is on economy, not menteri besar post
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 20 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has pledged to fully uphold the Johor State Constitution 1895 and the Sultan of Johor’s authority to appoint the Menteri Besar should the coalition be given the mandate to form the state government after the Johor state election. Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said PH did not want the Menteri Besar’s post to become the main focus of its campaign, as the coalition’s priority was to present policies and offers that would benefit the people. She was commenting on remarks by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who was reported to have urged PH to name its Menteri Besar candidate should the coalition succeed in forming the state government after the election.

items.title: ‘Greed is the main reason’: Kelantan police chief issues blunt warning over quick money scams
items.summary: Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investment scams recorded the highest financial losses among all commercial crime categories, with some victims losing more than RM1 million each. “Investment scams involve the highest losses. Some victims in Kelantan have lost more than RM1 million.

items.title: Over 1,000 media practitioners gather at PICCA Arena Butterworth for HAWANA 2026 finale
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, June 20 — The National Journalists’ Day (HAWANA) 2026 grand finale will be celebrated at the PICCA@Arena Butterworth Convention Centre here today, bringing together about 1,000 local and foreign media practitioners. Also expected to attend are Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow; Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil; Communications Ministry Secretary-General Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah; Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) Chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai; Bernama Chief Executive Officer Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin; as well as top management from local media organisations. Earlier, several programmes were organised in conjunction with HAWANA 2026, including the HAWANA 2026 Media Forum on May 7, HAWANA 2026 Strategic Partners Engagement Event on June 4, and HAWANA 2026 Fun Walk on June 14, which attracted encouraging participation from media practitioners and the public.

items.title: Minister Fadhlina calls for sensitivity and respect in case of Seremban student death
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 20 — Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek has urged the public to give space and respect the sensitivity of the family of a 16-year-old female student who died at a school here yesterday. “May the family be granted patience and strength in facing this very difficult test,” she said in a Facebook post yesterday. Earlier, Fadhlina and Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad visited the student’s family at Rembau Hospital to offer support.

items.title: Anwar conveys condolences over tragic death of Negeri Sembilan schoolgirl
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday conveyed his condolences to the family of a Form Four female student who died at a secondary school in Seremban, Negeri Sembilan, yesterday morning. Al-Fatihah," he said. Yesterday morning, Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said police received a report at 10:48 a.m. about the student being found unconscious at a secondary school and was pronounced dead at the scene.

items.title: Seremban school death: No evidence of bullying detected, says Education DG
items.summary: REMBAU, June 20 — No evidence of bullying has been detected so far in the case involving a Form Four female student who died at a secondary school in Seremban yesterday. Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said the 16-year-old student had reportedly been in good health and shown no unusual signs before the incident. “However, the state education department and the school have been instructed to thoroughly examine the case to determine the cause of the incident.

items.title: Police probe death of teenage girl found unconscious at Seremban school
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 20 — Police have confirmed that a 16-year-old female student died after being found unconscious at a secondary school here yesterday. Negeri Sembilan Police Chief Datuk Alzafny said the department received a report regarding the matter at 10:48 am, prompting the deployment of a team of police officers and personnel to the scene. Police conducted an inspection of the location and identified several individuals to assist with the investigation.

items.title: Amanah bets on youth and new blood in 19-seat bid for Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 20 — Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) will field 13 new faces as part of its 19-candidate lineup under the Pakatan Harapan (PH) banner for the upcoming Johor state election. “We (Amanah) will be contesting six seats in the northern part of Johor and five seats in the central part of the state. Also in attendance were Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan, deputy chairman Dzulkefly Ahmad, and vice-chairman Suhaizan Kaiat.

items.title: Storm tears through Ipoh, damages over 200 homes in Anjung Bercham (VIDEO)
items.summary: IPOH, June 19 — A violent afternoon storm ripped through Anjung Bercham here, damaging more than 200 homes across seven residential areas and triggering emergency response teams on the ground. “Acting on the information, three APM personnel were deployed to monitor the affected areas,” it said. Among the affected locations were: Dataran Tasek Timur 6 (177 houses) Hala Tasek Timur 1 (22 houses) Lorong Bercham 19 (22 houses) Hala Tasek Timur 34 (10 houses) Tasek Timur 15 (five houses) Lintasan Tasek Timur 1 (two houses) Lintasan Tasek Timur 3 (two houses) The Ipoh City Council (MBI) deployed emergency teams at 5.05pm to clear fallen trees and debris blocking roads and posing risks to residents.

items.title: JB nasi kandar shop suffers RM200,000 losses in pre-opening paint and arson attack
items.summary: Johor Bahru South district police chief ACP Raub Selamat said police received a report regarding the incident at 3.59 am from a 30-year-old local man who operates the restaurant and based on the restaurant’s closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, the two suspects arrived at about 3.34 am before carrying out the act. Meanwhile, Johor Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 3.43 am and dispatched personnel from the Tebrau Fire and Rescue Station and Kempas Fire and Rescue Station to the scene. However, the fire was successfully extinguished before the arrival of the fire and rescue team using eight fire extinguishers, and no injuries were reported.
